com.mentor.chs.api

Interface IXDiagram

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXGraphicConversions, IXObject, IXWriteableObject
    All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXAbstractLogicDiagram, IXFunctionDiagram, IXHarnessDiagram, IXLogicDiagram

    public interface IXDiagram
extends IXWriteableObject, IXGraphicConversions
    Interface representing the Diagram of a design.

    • Nested Class Summary

      • Nested classes/interfaces inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.api.IXGraphicConversions

        IXGraphicConversions.GenerateLegacySVG, IXGraphicConversions.XBaseContext, IXGraphicConversions.XCGMContext, IXGraphicConversions.XDXFContext, IXGraphicConversions.XPDFColorChoice, IXGraphicConversions.XPDFContext, IXGraphicConversions.XPDFMargins, IXGraphicConversions.XPDFPageSizeChoice, IXGraphicConversions.XPDFPaperOrientationChoice, IXGraphicConversions.XPDFPrintableAreaChoice, IXGraphicConversions.XPNGContext, IXGraphicConversions.XPNGDimensionContext, IXGraphicConversions.XPrintContext, IXGraphicConversions.XPrintDerivativeSelection, IXGraphicConversions.XPrintPDFContext, IXGraphicConversions.XPrintRevisionSelection, IXGraphicConversions.XPrintScope, IXGraphicConversions.XSVGContext

    • Method Detail

      • getZoneInfos

        java.util.Set<IXZoneInfo> getZoneInfos(IXObject object)
        Get the zone in which object is located in the diagram.
        Parameters:
        object - whose zone information need to be retrieved
        Returns:
        the zone if it exists, null otherwise.

      • getDiagramObjects

        java.util.Set<IXDiagramObject> getDiagramObjects(IXConnectivityObject connectivityObject)
        Get the set of diagram object whose connectivity is represented by parameter connectivityObject. This returns the diagram objects in this diagram only

        Example:

        The following example returns true if a pinlist has more than 3 representations in a diagram 
         boolean hasMoreThanOneDiagramObject(IXLogicDiagramPinList pinList) {
     IXDiagram diagram = pinlist.getDiagram();
        Set<IXLogicDiagramObject> diaObjs = diagram.getDiagramObjects(pinList.getConnectivity());
        return diaObjs.size() > 3;
 }
        Parameters:
        connectivityObject - whose corresponding diagram objects need to be retrieved
        Returns:
        a Set of diagram objects.

      • getUnitDistance

        double getUnitDistance()
        Get the unit of the physical mapping of the diagram in terms of Meter. If unit is mm it will return 0.001, if unit is cm it will return 0.01, if unit is inch it will return 0.0254. If the value returned is 0.05 then it means that 1 unit of this mapping is 0.05 meters. -1.0 is returned in case of any failure to get the value.
        Returns:
        unit in double

      • getDesign

        IXDesign getDesign()
        Get the design to which the diagram belongs.
        Returns:
        the design to which diagram belongs.

      • getAssociatedObjects

        <O extends IXDiagramBaseObject> java.util.Set<O> getAssociatedObjects(java.lang.Class<O> filterClass)
        Get all the graphical/diagram objects associated to this diagram, based on the filter. The filter class enables the retrieval of only those associated objects that are of a specified type.

        For Instance, for a Diagram containing a PinList and a Conductor, the PinList graphics, the Conductor graphics and the Comment graphics(if any) are the diagram's associated objects.

        Also, a getAssociatedObjects call on an IXLogicDiagram with IXLogicDiagramPinList.class as a filter would retrieve all the schematic pin lists contained in the logic diagram.

        Parameters:
        filterClass - the class based on which the associated objects of this diagram would be filtered to contain only the objects of this class type in the return set.
        Returns:
        a set of diagram objects. This set is never null and unmodifable.

      • getStyleSetName

        java.lang.String getStyleSetName()
        Returns:
        The name of the style set associated with the diagram.
